preview_text:
The model favors France at 84% to win, leaving Iraq with less than 1% probability of victory. The gap reflects a large expected asymmetry in quality or circumstance. Iraq's underdog position is pronounced under all three probability sources.

The model assigns a 16% chance to a draw, the highest probability outcome for any single result apart from a France win. This split suggests France are favored but not overwhelmingly so in absolute terms; competitive matches often settle via draws, and this one carries meaningful probability of that outcome. A France win remains the clear baseline, yet the match is not modeled as a formality.

The model and sportsbooks diverge noticeably on Iraq's chances. The model puts them at 0.8%, while the market consensus sits near 3%, a gap of roughly 2 percentage points. The market is also more bullish on France outright (89–90%) and more skeptical of draws (7–8%) than the model. The dashboard does not adjudicate which reading is more accurate; users may weigh both assessments in their own forecasts.
